summary: Backend-only testing of raw material rate recalculation feature for Puresh Daily Plant Operation. Verified that creating/updating a raw material rate auto-recalculates cost_per_unit in all batches and raw_material_stock entries within the rate's active window, and that batch totals (total_raw_material_cost, cost_per_unit) correctly reflect the updated costs. All 6 pytest tests PASSED against https://daily-plant-ops.preview.emergentagent.com.
critical_code_review_comments: recalculate_batches_for_material() in raw_material_routes.py correctly updates both batches and raw_material_stock for affected date windows. Logic is sound.
update_raw_material_rate enforces latest-rate-only edits and then invokes recalculation -- good.

create_raw_material_rate auto-closes previous rate by setting its to_date to new_from - 1 day. Verified to work (sugar's older rate correctly has to_date 2026-04-17 while new rate from 2026-04-18 is latest).

Minor perf: recalculate_batches_for_material loops all batches and stock entries for the material; at large scale consider filtering by date window via aggregation. Not a blocker at current data volume.

compute_batch_costs derives totals on fetch from stored cost_per_unit -- so only stored per-batch cost_per_unit needs updating. The tests confirm both the stored per-batch value and derived totals update correctly after a rate change.
No rate-effective-date validation on create/update: overlapping rates or rates with from_date > to_date are accepted silently. Consider enforcing constraints (already partially done via auto-close).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Tests use existing batch (042617A with sugar) and (032630A with SMP). Test flow is non-destructive: rate PUT value is mutated then restored; rate POST uses a far-future from_date (2099-01-01) and is deleted + previous rate restored after test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_rate_recalculation.py covers: (1) PUT /api/raw-material-rate/{id} -> batches & stock within window get cost_per_unit updated; (2) POST /api/raw-material-rate -> previous latest rate auto-closed to new_from - 1 day, new rate created, batches in prior window unchanged; (3) GET /api/raw-material-rates-all returns expected shape; (4) GET /api/batches returns paginated object with 'batches' key. Note: batches API returns a paginated object {batches: [...], pagination: {...}}, not a bare list.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: cost_per_unit stored on each batch's raw_materials[] element was never re-read from rates after a rate change. Fix (verified): raw_material_routes.recalculate_batches_for_material() scans all rates for the material, iterates all batches/stock with that material name, and updates each entry's cost_per_unit to the rate active on that date. Called from both create_raw_material_rate and update_raw_material_rate. compute_batch_costs() then derives total_raw_material_cost and cost_per_unit from the freshly-updated stored cost_per_unit values on each GET. End-to-end verified: changing sugar's latest rate (46.5) via PUT updates batch 042617A's sugar cost_per_unit and total_raw_material_cost; creating a future rate correctly auto-closes the previous rate's to_date to (new_from - 1 day) without touching batches outside the new window.
